text:
There once was a young boy named Max. He loved building things out of wood, antlers, and sometimes even old bicycle wheels. One summer afternoon, Max decided to build a small treehouse. He collected branches, branches, branches... you get the idea.

The treehouse was surprisingly cozy. The floor was stuffed with fluffy pillows made from old fabric, the walls were plastered with pictures he found in old magazines, and the roof was covered in plastic stars. But what really made it their home was a small fireplace.

One rainy afternoon, Max sat by the fireplace, nursing a mug of tea. Outside, rain droplets tapped a rhythm against the windowpane. Inside, the crackling fire danced, sending tiny stars into the room.

That night, a raccoon wandered into the forest. It noticed the bright plastic stars flickering away from the firelight. The creature fancied themselves somewhat knowledgeable about stars. It approached cautiously. Suddenly the tiny stars twinkled softly.

The creature paused
